title: salix occidentalis var. longipes
intro: Salix occidentalis var. longipes, also known as the western willow, is a variety of willow tree that is native to North America. It is a shrub or small tree with slender branches and narrow leaves that has been used for its medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of the western willow contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and relieve fever. The herbal preparations of Salix occidentalis var. longipes, such as teas, salves, and tinctures, have been used to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including arthritis, headaches, and menstrual cramps.
